v100_primary.gpus: Tesla V100-PCIE-32GB x6 (shared; UAP training used GPU 5 or 4,2)

pro6000_secondary.gpus: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition x2 (sm_120)

training_settings.distractor_pool: SA-V test split (real, 150 clips), sampled 30 per step

code_fixes_vs_public_release: R1 fix 1: grad(loss, adv_img, loss) -> grad(loss, adv_img)[0] (scalar grad_outputs bug)
R1 fix 2: dropped sample_total_g/sample_step_count history averaging; per-step EMA only
R2: strict sav_NNNNNN regex whitelist + empty-folder preflight + mkdir exist_ok for uap_file
torch.no_grad() wraps on constant-feature paths (target_feature, prototype_feature, clean logits+out, adv out) for V100 32GB fit
Removed aggressive per-frame del + empty_cache() that caused CUDA illegal memory access with SAM2 memory bank
deviations_from_strict_paper_protocol: Exact 100-video training subset not reproducible from public artifacts (paper does not publish the list; we use seed=30 sample from YT-VOS train)
Paper's undocumented hyperparameters (schedule, number of epochs) not known
Pro 6000 host has different driver/pytorch - identical eval code, same UAP tensor; results should be deterministic modulo arch differences

uap_file/YOUTUBE.pth.description: Original public-release optimizer path (pre-fixes), no loss_fea

uap_file/YOUTUBE_v2.pth.description: Codex-fixed optimizer (R1+R2 fixes applied) + real SA-V distractors + all losses enabled

uap_file/YOUTUBE_fixed.pth.description: Intermediate (uap_attack_fixed.py on 2026-04-02); NEVER EVALUATED on either protocol; flags unknown; superseded by YOUTUBE_v2.pth

in_domain_train_100.description: Same 100 train videos used for UAP optimization; overlap_with_train=100/100

v2_vs_v1_improvement_pp.interpretation: Codex's R1/R2 fixes + real SA-V distractors genuinely strengthen the attack under both protocols.

paper_targets.protocol_note: Paper's headline appears to be in-domain (tested on training videos per public code)

gap_to_paper.status: Our current reproduction is incomplete; gap to paper remains unexplained. Not a claim that the paper is unreproducible. Continue investigating.
gap_to_paper.interpretation: SAM2 model, data pipeline, and eval protocol are correct. The unexplained attack-strength gap is on the training side only.
